data.title: Bridgewater Associates adds new stakes in CAT, DELL, exits T, NLY, among Q4 moves
data.description:
Bridgewater Associates, the hedge fund manager founded by billionaire investor Ray Dalio, took new positions in Caterpillar (CAT), with about 62.2K shares, Dell Technologies (DELL), with 156.3K shares, and Spotify Technology (SPOT), with 23.5K shares, during Q4 2025, according to a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing released on Friday.

Conversely, the firm closed out its holdings in AT&T (T), Annaly Capital Management (NLY), Affirm Holdings (AFRM) and Aehr test Systems (AEHR.

Increased stakes include Nvidia (NVDA), to 3.87M shares in Q4 from 2.51M shares in the prior quarter, Amazon (AMZN), to 1.95M from 1.12M, and Mastercard (MA), to 409.2K from 366.3K, the 13F filing showed.

Trimmed stakes: Salesforce (CRM), to 1.93M shares in Q4 from 2.01M in Q3, Microsoft (MSFT), to 984K from 1.10M, and Wells Fargo (WFC), to 1.14M from 2.54M.

data.title: China's supercharged gold trading looks like 'speculative bubble,' Capital Economics says
data.description:
China's recent gold trading frenzy appears more like a speculative bubble than a typical flight to safe-haven assets, raising the risk of more market turbulence, Capital Economics analysts said Friday.

"The growing use of leverage and futures trading to gain exposure to gold in China suggests that the recent increase in Chinese gold demand is more consistent with a speculative bubble inflating," analyst Hamad Hussain said in a note.

Private Chinese investor demand is a major driver of gold prices, fueling greater use of gold-linked financial products, and the increase in gold warrants on the Shanghai Futures Exchange also indicates that futures trading has become a bigger part of China's gold market, Hussain explained.

The continuing property downturn should support safe-haven demand for gold in China in the medium term, Hussain added, but he also noted that Chinese investors are increasingly using leverage to gain exposure to gold, which could boost market volatility and the likelihood of sharp price swings like those seen earlier this year.

Gold and silver prices jumped on Friday, as weaker-than-expected U.S. inflation results reignited hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, offsetting concerns from stronger-than-expected jobs data earlier in the week.

"The broader backdrop remains one of elevated volatility following this week's sharp liquidation across precious metals, but today's move suggests the correction may have overshot, with bargain hunting and position adjustments now providing support," ING commodity strategist Ewa Manthey said in a note.

On Friday, front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February delivery closed +2% to $5,022.00/oz, and front-month Comex February silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +3% to $77.851/oz.

Gold and silver both rose 1.4% this week; gold has gained in eight out of the past 10 weeks, and silver has finished higher in 11 out of the past 15 weeks.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Bank of America pays CEO Brian Moynihan $41M for 2025
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) increased the total 2025 compensation for Chair and CEO Brian Moynihan by 17% from $35M in 2024, the company disclosed in a filing on Friday.

The board considered the company's 2025 performance, including 13% net income growth, 7% revenue growth, its balance sheet strength, expense management, low net charge-off ratio, and shareholder returns in determining his compensation.

During 2025, Bank of America (BAC) stock rose 25%, compared with the S&P 500's 16% increase.

His annual base salary is $1.5M, consistent with previous years. He received no cash bonus. The total value of his 2025 equity incentive award is $39.5M, made up of 30% of cash-settled restricted stock units that will vest over the next 12 months, 20% of stock-settled RSUs that will vest annually over the next four years, and 50% of stock-settled performance RSUs.

data.title: X3 Holdings faces Nasdaq delisting over sub-$1 share price
data.description:
• X3 Holdings Co (XTKG) received a Nasdaq determination letter on February 10, citing non-compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • Although Nasdaq typically provides a 180-day compliance period, X3 is not eligible due to its one-for-six reverse stock split completed on December 30, 2025.

  • Nasdaq has determined to delist the company’s securities from The Nasdaq Capital Market.

  • Unless X3 requests an appeal by February 17, 2026, trading will be suspended at market open on February 19, 2026.

  • XTKG shares down 5% post-market.

data.title: Disc Medicine plunges on FDA Complete Response Letter for bitopertin
data.description:
• Disc Medicine (IRON) closed down ~22% Friday after receiving a Complete Response Letter from the US FDA for its application for bitopertin as a treatment for erythropoietic protoporphyria.
  • The agency noted it will need to see the results of the phase 3 APOLLO trial before making a final decision. Topline data is expected in Q4.
  • Disc's NDA included data from the phase 2 AURORA and BEACON trials. The company noted that the FDA found the results from these trials indicated bitopertin led to significantly lowered levels of PPIX, the molecule in the skin that causes the condition.
  • Bitopertin received accelerated approval review and is one of the candidates under the FDA Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher pilot program.
  • Erythropoietic protoporphyria is a rare, inherited condition characterized by severe skin photosensitivity.

data.title: Sabra outlines 4.9% to 5.4% FFO growth for 2026 while accelerating SHOP investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sabra Health Care REIT, Inc. (SBRA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Rick Matros opened by highlighting that "Sabra's NOI growth for the SHOP portfolio, excluding our transition facilities, is expected to be sturdy in 2026 as it has been in 2025, and we expect the transition facilities as they continue to improve to add to the overall growth in our SHOP performance." Matros noted that the company's guidance of 4.9% and 5.4% growth at the midpoint for normalized FFO and normalized AFFO, respectively, reflects ongoing execution of strategy. He reported the investment pipeline remains robust, with $450 million completed in 2025 and $240 million in awarded deals expected to close in early 2026. Matros emphasized, "Our expectation is that we will materially exceed the volume of 2025 investments and are clearly off to a strong start in 2026."
  • Matros emphasized operational performance across the portfolio, including "strong occupancy gains and increased cash NOI margins" in the SHOP segment, margin improvement in same-store Senior Housing, and all-time high rent coverage for skilled nursing. Leverage was reported as steady at the 5x target, and the regulatory environment remains stable.
  • Darrin Smith, Chief Investment Officer, reported sequential revenue growth of 15.8% and cash NOI growth of 18.4% with a margin expansion of 60 basis points in the managed Senior Housing portfolio. Smith stated, "During the quarter, Sabra invested over $150 million, adding 4 properties to Sabra's managed portfolio, bringing total year investments to roughly $450 million with an estimated initial cash yield of 7.5%."
  • CFO Michael Costa reported, "For the fourth quarter of 2025, we recognized normalized FFO per share of $0.36, and normalized AFFO per share of $0.38. In absolute dollars, normalized FFO and normalized AFFO totaled $91.2 million and $95.2 million this quarter, respectively."

Outlook

  • Costa introduced full-year 2026 guidance: Net income per share of $0.60 to $0.64, FFO and normalized FFO per share of $1.49 to $1.53, and AFFO and normalized AFFO per share of $1.55 to $1.59. He stated, "At the midpoint, we expect both normalized FFO per share and normalized AFFO per share to increase approximately 5% over 2025."
  • Costa explained that guidance assumes cash NOI growth for the triple-net portfolio in the low single digits, with same-store managed Senior Housing portfolio cash NOI growth in the low to mid-teens.
  • Guidance does not assume any 2026 investment, disposition, or capital markets activities that have not yet been completed.

Financial Results

  • Costa reported that cash NOI from the triple-net portfolio decreased $1.3 million from the third quarter, while managed Senior Housing portfolio cash NOI increased $5.5 million, leading to a net sequential increase of $4.2 million. Managed Senior Housing portfolio cash NOI totaled $35.6 million in Q4 compared to $30.1 million in Q3.
  • Interest and other income was $10.6 million for the quarter, down from $12.7 million last quarter. Cash interest expense was $26.6 million, consistent with the previous quarter. Cash G&A was $12.5 million, up from $9.1 million, with the increase attributed to performance-based compensation.
  • Costa highlighted a net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio of 5.00x as of December 31, 2025, and a permanent debt cost of 3.92% with a weighted average remaining term of 4.2 years. Liquidity stood at approximately $1.2 billion.
  • The Board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.30 per share, representing a payout of 79% of normalized AFFO per share.

Q&A

  • William John Kilichowski, Wells Fargo: Asked about building blocks for same-store growth and long-term prospects. Darrin Smith responded, "We expect to see continued occupancy growth...we fully expect our portfolio to get into the low 90s."
  • Kilichowski asked about the maturity of the RCA loan and capital redeployment. Matros responded that the loan is being serviced and guidance assumes the lease stays in place.
  • Juan Sanabria, BMO: Asked about CapEx expectations. Smith estimated non-maintenance CapEx in 2026 to be in the $20 million to $30 million range.
  • Michael Goldsmith, UBS: Inquired about occupancy targets. Matros said, "We think we can exceed 90%. Once you get to the mid-90s, you're kind of effectively full."
  • Austin Wurschmidt, KeyBanc: Asked about transition assets' contribution to NOI. Costa explained that transition assets are currently lagging but expected to improve and provide additional NOI uplift.
  • Seth Bergey, Citi: Asked about investment mix and competitive landscape. Costa confirmed $240 million of awarded transactions is significantly weighted towards SHOP, with return expectations unchanged.
  • Michael Stroyeck, Green Street: Asked about non-same-store SHOP asset growth. Smith stated newer assets still have room for NOI growth before inclusion in the same-store pool.
  • Farrell Granath, BofA: Asked about acquisition occupancy levels and NOI margin potential. Matros said assets are typically acquired at 86-87% occupancy, providing room for growth.
  • Alec Feygin, Baird: Asked about deal flow competition and development lending. Costa noted cap rate compression but continued access to quality assets and described a preferred equity program for development.
  • Omotayo Okusanya, Deutsche Bank: Inquired about skilled nursing regulatory outlook. Matros responded that rate increases are tapering but remain robust, with no state-level concerns.
  • Richard Anderson, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about SHOP operational advantages. Matros highlighted Sabra's experienced asset management team and continuous process improvement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was inquisitive and focused on long-term growth drivers, risk management, and portfolio composition, with a neutral to slightly positive sentiment. Questions sought clarification on occupancy, investment pipeline, and operational execution.
  • Management's tone in prepared remarks was confident, emphasizing robust performance and future growth. During Q&A, responses remained consistent, providing detailed explanations and affirming guidance, with Matros and Costa displaying steady assurance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained a similar focus but with increased attention to execution and future growth runway. Management's confidence remained steady, reinforced by strong operational and financial trends.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 introduces a 4.9% to 5.4% growth target for normalized FFO and AFFO, compared to reaffirmed 2025 guidance in the previous quarter.
  • Strategic focus remains on expanding the SHOP portfolio, with investment activity accelerating from $450 million in 2025 to expectations of surpassing that in 2026.
  • Analysts' questions continue to focus on occupancy, investment mix, and operational execution, while management maintains confidence and provides more granularity on strategic initiatives and guidance assumptions.
  • Key metrics such as managed Senior Housing occupancy, margin improvement, and pipeline growth continue to be highlighted, with operational performance sustaining momentum.

Risks and Concerns

  • Matros noted that guidance does not assume new investments beyond those completed or awarded, potentially limiting upside if pipeline deals are delayed.
  • Smith acknowledged that non-maintenance CapEx could range from $20 million to $30 million, indicating continued reinvestment needs.
  • Analyst concerns centered on transition assets' lag in performance, regulatory risks in skilled nursing, and competitive pressures in asset acquisitions. Management emphasized stable regulatory environment and disciplined investment approach.

Final Takeaway

Sabra Health Care REIT enters 2026 with robust operational momentum, guided by a strong investment pipeline and a focus on expanding its SHOP portfolio. Management projects mid-single-digit growth in normalized FFO and AFFO, supported by occupancy gains, margin expansion, and disciplined capital deployment, while maintaining a stable regulatory outlook and substantial liquidity. The company expects continued growth in both organic and external avenues, with a clear path to exceeding prior investment volumes and reinforcing its strategic positioning in senior housing and healthcare real estate.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Baupost adds Amazon, exits Restaurant Brands, boosts UNP stake, among Q4 trades
data.description:
Seth Klarman's Baupost hedge fund added Amazon (AMZN), exited its stake in Restaurant Brands International, and increased its holdings in Union Pacific (UNP), among other Q4 tradings disclosed in its 13 filing.

The fund added 2.12M shares of Amazon (AMNZ) valued at $489.7M, 625K shares of  Molina Healthcare (MOH) ($108.5M), and 4.86M shares of Grupo Aeromexico (AERO) ($106.6M).

It closed its holdings in Restaurant Brands International (QSR).

The firm increased its holdings in Union Pacific (UNP) to 1.63M shares ($376.4M) from 1.50M shares in the prior quarter; Fidelity National Information (FIS) to 4.50M ($299.3M) shares from 3.79M; and Fiserv (FI) to 2.20M shares ($147.8M) from 895K shares.

During the quarter, Baupost reduced its stakes in Alphabet (GOOG) class C shares to 1.09M shares ($341.2M) from 1.86M shares; CRH (CRH) to 1.08M shares ($113.9M) from 3.83M shares; and Liberty Global (LBTYA) class A shares to 2.08M ($23.2M) from 5.08M shares, among others, according to the filing.

data.title: Visionary Holdings secures $450,000 in debt financing
data.description:
• Visionary Holdings (GV) has entered into a securities purchase agreement with an existing institutional investor, under which it has issued to the Investor a senior secured convertible promissory note in the principal amount of $500,000 in exchange for gross proceeds of $450,000.
  • Previously the company had issued to the investor a senior secured convertible promissory note in the principal amount of $1.50M pursuant to a securities purchase agreement dated October 2, 2024.
  • GV +6.09% after hours to $1.22.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: J&J hit with $250K jury verdict in Philadelphia talc powder case
data.description:
• A jury in a Philadelphia court found Johnson & Johnson liable for a woman developing ovarian cancer after using the healthcare giant's talc-based powder.
  • The estate of Gayle Emerson was awarded $50K in compensatory damages and $200K in punitive damages.
  • Emerson used J&J's powder for over 45 years and was diagnosed with the cancer in 2015 when she was 65, according to testimony during the trial. She died four years later.

data.title: Longeveron appoints new CEO
data.description:
• Longeveron Inc. (LGVN) has appointed Stephen H. Willard as chief executive officer, effective February 11, 2026.
  • Willard succeeds Than Powell, who had been serving as interim CEO.
